content: Determinism breaks when: (1) Math.random() is called anywhere in synthesis — seeded PRNGs like mulberry32 or xoshiro128** must replace all RNG calls; (2) RNG call count or order diverges between client and server — identical input must produce identical call sequence; (3) floating-point results differ across CPU/compiler — mitigate by rounding coordinates with Math.floor and avoiding transcendental functions where possible; (4) JSON.stringify key ordering is relied upon for hashing — use sorted key serialization; (5) date/time APIs differ between environments (Date.now() vs injected timestamp). Lockstep multiplayer engines use fixed-point math to avoid float drift entirely.
